module-0-student-roster-project.label: Module 0 Student Roster Project
module-0-student-roster-project.summary: Build a simple student roster that becomes more useful with each step.

mp-1-preview-student-roster.title: Preview the roster columns
mp-1-preview-student-roster.prompt: Begin the Module 0 Student Roster Project. Return `id, name, grade` from `students` in that column order.
mp-1-preview-student-roster.hint: Add the missing field in the SELECT list.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, grade` from `students` in that column order.

mp-1-preview-student-roster.starterCode:
-- Returning id, name, and grade from the students table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM students;

mp-2-add-city-to-roster.title: Add each student's city
mp-2-add-city-to-roster.prompt: Extend the Module 0 Student Roster Project with the next approved requirement. Return `id, name, grade, city` from `students` in that column order.
mp-2-add-city-to-roster.hint: Extend the SELECT list instead of starting over.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, grade, city` from `students` in that column order.

mp-2-add-city-to-roster.starterCode:
-- Keep the roster fields above.
-- Add city before FROM students.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  grade
FROM students;

mp-3-finish-student-roster.title: Finish the full roster view
mp-3-finish-student-roster.prompt: Complete the Module 0 Student Roster Project. Return `id, name, grade, age, city` from `students` in that column order.
mp-3-finish-student-roster.hint: Insert one more field into the SELECT list.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, grade, age, city` from `students` in that column order.

mp-3-finish-student-roster.starterCode:
-- Keep the roster fields above.
-- Add age between grade and city.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  grade,
  city
FROM students;

try_module_0_student_roster_project_sket.title: Preview id and name from students
try_module_0_student_roster_project_sket.prompt: Scenario: The school office is learning how to read its student roster database. Write a query that returns `id` and `name` from the `students` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.

module-0-student-roster-project.label: Module 0 Student Roster Project
module-0-student-roster-project.summary: Build a simple student roster that becomes more useful with each step.

mp-1-preview-student-roster.title: Preview the roster columns
mp-1-preview-student-roster.prompt: Start the student roster by returning `id`, `name`, and `grade` from the `students` table.
mp-1-preview-student-roster.hint: Add the missing field in the SELECT list.

help.concept: This step adds one more detail to the roster so it is easier to use.
help.hint_1: Keep `id` and `name`, then add `grade` before `FROM students`.
help.hint_2: Run the query and check that the result shows three columns in that order.

mp-1-preview-student-roster.starterCode:
-- Returning id, name, and grade from the students table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM students;

mp-2-add-city-to-roster.title: Add each student's city
mp-2-add-city-to-roster.prompt: Add the `city` column so the roster shows where each student is from.
mp-2-add-city-to-roster.hint: Extend the SELECT list instead of starting over.

help.concept: Add the new detail without changing the parts of the roster that already work.
help.hint_1: Keep `id`, `name`, and `grade` exactly as they are.
help.hint_2: Add `city` before `FROM students`, then rerun the query.

mp-2-add-city-to-roster.starterCode:
-- Keep the roster fields above.
-- Add city before FROM students.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  grade
FROM students;

mp-3-finish-student-roster.title: Finish the full roster view
mp-3-finish-student-roster.prompt: Add `age` so the final roster returns `id`, `name`, `grade`, `age`, and `city` from `students`.
mp-3-finish-student-roster.hint: Insert one more field into the SELECT list.

help.concept: This last step completes the roster with the final missing field.
help.hint_2: Add `age` between `grade` and `city`, then rerun the query.

mp-3-finish-student-roster.starterCode:
-- Keep the roster fields above.
-- Add age between grade and city.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  grade,
  city
FROM students;

try-module-0-student-roster-project-sket.title: Preview id and name from students
try-module-0-student-roster-project-sket.prompt: Scenario: The school office is learning how to read its student roster database. Write a query that returns `id` and `name` from the `students` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
try-module-0-student-roster-project-sket.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson, then adjust the selected columns, table, or filter for this scenario.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the selected columns and table name in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the result table with the expected result.

try-module-0-student-roster-project-sket.starterCode:
-- Return id and name from the students table.
SELECT
  id
FROM students;

try_module_0_student_roster_project_sket.title: Preview id and name from students
try_module_0_student_roster_project_sket.prompt: Scenario: The school office is learning how to read its student roster database. Write a query that returns `id` and `name` from the `students` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
The school office is preparing a roster snapshot for the first week of classes.

Start with a simple student list, then add the details that make the roster useful at a glance.

The finished result should present each student's key information in one clean view.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
The school office is preparing a roster snapshot for the first week of classes.

Start with a simple student list, then add the details that make the roster useful at a glance.

The finished result should present each student's key information in one clean view.
